author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! kibā tumi vicāri nā cāo
‘kṛṣṇa’ bhuli ei pāpa, te̐ha tora tina tāpa,
nānā joni bhramiyā beḍāo

tumi kṛṣṇa⋅nityadāsa, kothā gela se abhyāsa,
dhana⋅jana⋅made haiyā āndhe
vināmūlye māthā pāti, dāsa haye khāo lāthi,
śraddhāte vasana diyā kāndhe

ei mora sadā dhanda, kaha lakṣa kathā manda,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’ loite ālisa
thākite rasanā tuṇḍa, jāo kena naraka⋅kuṇḍa,
ihā haite ke āra bāliśa

vṛthā tabe nara⋅tanu, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhajana vinu,
kemane pāmara jīte cāya
kṛṣṇa vinā koṭiyuga, jīyei vā kon sukha,
se jīvana pātharera prāya

ebāra mānuṣa⋅deha, āra ki hoibe eha,
bhaja kṛṣṇa, chāḍa anācāra
dekha saba nāśā⋅phā̐dā, kevala anartha⋅dhā̐dhā,
asamaye haya kebā kā’ra

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha anukṣaṇa,
āpanāra tattve hoo daḍha
saṁsāra⋅vāsanā⋅garta, kīṭa⋅krimimaya kata,
dekhiyā śuniyā kena paḍa
en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you not consider this? Forgetting Lord Kṛṣṇa is the root of all sin; it brings you the three-fold miseries and makes you wander through various species.

You are an eternal servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa—where has that understanding gone? Becoming blind with the intoxication of wealth and family, you bow your head to illusion without any gain, becoming its slave and receiving only kicks.

This is my constant confusion: you speak a million useless words, but you are lazy to utter the Name of Kṛṣṇa. While having a tongue in your mouth, why do you choose to go to the pit of hell? Who could be more foolish than this?

This human body is useless without the worship of Lord Kṛṣṇa. How can a wretched soul even desire to live like this? Without Lord Kṛṣṇa, what happiness is there in living for millions of ages? Such a life is like a stone.

You have this human body now—will you ever get it again? Worship Lord Kṛṣṇa and give up all unrighteousness. See all these destructive snares and useless puzzles of this world. In the end, who will truly belong to whom?

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant the Name of Kṛṣṇa constantly and become firm in your own spiritual identity. Why do you fall into the pit of material desires, full of worms and insects, even after seeing and hearing the truth?"
